name: Pharmacogenomics (PGx)
description: Drug-metabolism variants that affect dosing and response. Cross-referenced with PharmGKB/CPIC.

snps.description: Affects clopidogrel, PPIs, citalopram, escitalopram, some SSRIs.

AA.note: Poor metabolizer — clopidogrel may be ineffective; SSRIs may accumulate.

CT.note: Rapid metabolizer — drugs cleared faster than expected.

TT.note: Ultra-rapid metabolizer — standard doses may be subtherapeutic.

CT.note: Intermediate — bleeding risk with warfarin, consider dose reduction.

TT.note: Poor metabolizer — significant bleeding risk with warfarin.

snps.description: More severe variant; affects same drug set as *2.

AC.note: Intermediate — significant dose reduction often needed.

snps.description: Warfarin's target enzyme; variants increase sensitivity.

TT.note: High sensitivity — lowest starting dose band.

snps.description: Modestly increases warfarin dose requirement; most useful when combined with CYP2C9 and VKORC1.

CC.note: Baseline CYP4F2 contribution to warfarin dosing.

CT.note: Variant present — some patients need a modestly higher warfarin dose than CYP2C9/VKORC1 alone would predict.

TT.note: Strongest common CYP4F2 dose-increase signal; pair with CYP2C9 and VKORC1 rather than reading in isolation.

snps.description: Controls statin uptake into hepatocytes; risk variant raises simvastatin-induced myopathy risk.

CC.note: Highest myopathy risk — avoid simvastatin 80 mg.

snps.description: Common BCRP transporter variant. Most relevant for rosuvastatin exposure and complements SLCO1B1 for statin interpretation.

CC.note: Baseline ABCG2 transporter activity at this site.

AC.note: Decreased transporter function — rosuvastatin exposure can run higher than expected.

AA.note: Poorer ABCG2 transporter function — strongest common signal here for elevated rosuvastatin exposure.

snps.description: Rare but highly actionable fluoropyrimidine toxicity variant for 5-FU and capecitabine.

GG.note: No DPYD *2A variant detected at this site.

AG.note: Actionable DPYD toxicity variant present — fluoropyrimidines can cause severe toxicity without dose reduction.

AA.note: Two DPYD *2A risk alleles present — extremely high fluoropyrimidine toxicity risk.

snps.description: Rare DPYD loss-of-function variant with strong fluoropyrimidine toxicity relevance.

TT.note: No DPYD *13 variant detected at this site.

TG.note: Actionable DPYD loss-of-function variant present — major fluoropyrimidine dose reduction is typically required.

GG.note: Two DPYD *13 risk alleles present — strongest toxicity signal in this marker set.

snps.description: Clinically actionable DPYD decreased-function variant associated with fluoropyrimidine toxicity.

AA.note: No DPYD c.2846A>T variant detected at this site.

AT.note: Actionable DPYD toxicity variant present — standard 5-FU or capecitabine doses can be unsafe.

TT.note: Two DPYD c.2846A>T risk alleles present — strongest common toxicity signal at this site.

snps.description: Tag SNP used on some arrays for the DPYD HapB3 decreased-function haplotype. Useful, but not as definitive as directly assaying the causal intronic SNP.

GG.note: No common HapB3 tag signal detected at this site.

AG.note: HapB3 tag present — possible DPYD decreased function. Confirm with a clinical PGx assay before treatment decisions.

AA.note: Two HapB3 tag alleles are present. This is a stronger common signal for lower DPD activity, but still benefits from confirmatory testing.

snps.description: Thiopurine metabolism — azathioprine, 6-MP, 6-TG. Poor metabolizers face severe bone marrow toxicity at standard doses.

TT.note: Poor metabolizer — standard thiopurine doses can cause fatal myelosuppression. Requires dose reduction or alternative therapy.

snps.description: Tacrolimus, some statins. Expressors need higher doses than non-expressors.

TT.note: Non-expressor (common in European ancestry) — standard dosing.

CT.note: Intermediate expresser — dose adjustment may be needed.
